h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: Sorry, I keep hearing the red mark as 'broken,' like people say at work
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think the new way to read it is that "be" has a descriptive rule here
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply identify the failing sentence as requested by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the circled p-value was enough to carry the recommendation, because that is usually the flag I look for first.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner proceeds to apply the p-value/estimate distinction to the specific cost question asked in T2.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought a canyon this big almost had to mean one huge flood, or something violent like that.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that gives me something to check instead of just staring at the size of the canyon.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue expressing uncertainty about how to visually identify the geological processes without a specific testing method.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought "presumed innocent" meant the court was saying, at least until later, that the accused basically did not do it.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That was the problem, I think
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would simply accept the tutor's corrected sentence and proceed to write it on the handout.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought removing wolves would simply mean more deer and a healthier herd.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns establish a trajectory of correcting a factual misconception about deer health through direct instruction and immediate application.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought, because the water is clear now, the salt had gone, or was not really there in the beaker any more.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Now it sounds more like the grains have vanished, but the salt still remains in the beaker and on the balance.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The dialogue was proceeding toward a direct answer to the tutor's logbook question without looking back.
